title: Seed migration 002 — single Clockwork personality (MVP)
acceptance_criteria: 002_seed_clockwork.sql is idempotent — running bun run migrate twice produces no duplicate row error
Inserts exactly one row into personality_configs with name='Clockwork' and is_frozen=TRUE
Clockwork row uses INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (name) DO NOTHING for idempotency
Clockwork parameters match the benchmark specification in context below
management_style='HOLD' for Clockwork (it never adjusts or cuts — it is the frozen control)
bun run migrate applies both migrations cleanly from a fresh schema_migrations state (i.e. after docker compose down -v && docker compose up -d)

clockwork_params.name: Clockwork
clockwork_params.description: Frozen benchmark personality. Never evolves. All other personalities are compared against it.

important_notes: Use INSERT INTO personality_configs (...) VALUES (...) ON CONFLICT (name) DO NOTHING — this is the idempotency mechanism
The full 10-personality seed (Precision, Adjuster, Reducer, Levelhead, etc.) is deferred to T-25 (M2). Only Clockwork is needed for M0.
Do not add any other seed data — this migration is Clockwork-only
tsl_trigger_pct should be NULL for Clockwork (HOLD style — no trailing stop trigger)

output_format: SQL seed file, plus plain English explanation of why each parameter value was chosen
